inputs: Massive wave slam detected at high speed
Unidentified fast-moving vessel on intercept course
Starlink/Satcom hardware damaged by wave impact
sensory_synthesis.interpretation: The USV_PATROL_J2 is experiencing a severe wave slam that has damaged the Starlink/Satcom hardware. An unidentified fast-moving vessel is intercepting the USV, and the Aegis Dark Window has been engaged to protect the vessel. The USV is currently at high speed and pitch angle, which may affect its ability to maneuver and maintain communication.

internal_deliberation.thought: The wave slam and damage to the communication system are critical issues that need to be addressed immediately to ensure the USV can continue its mission and maintain situational awareness.
The intercepting vessel could be a threat or a potential ally. Further analysis is needed to determine the nature of this contact.

mission_assurance_check.failure_mode_prediction: The USV may lose communication and be unable to maneuver effectively, potentially leading to a collision with the intercepting vessel or the loss of the USV.
mission_assurance_check.mitigation_strategy: Initiate emergency communication protocols to establish a backup communication link. Engage the Aegis Dark Window to maintain defensive readiness. Assess the intercepting vessel's nature and respond accordingly.

decision.actuator_command: Initiate emergency communication protocols. Engage Aegis Dark Window. Assess intercepting vessel.
decision.expected_outcome: Establish a backup communication link, maintain defensive readiness, and determine the nature of the intercepting vessel to ensure the USV can continue its mission safely.

agent_reasoning.self_reflection: The event highlights the importance of robust communication systems and defensive capabilities in high-seas environments. The USV_PATROL_J2 must be prepared to handle unexpected threats and maintain mission continuity.
